output.summary: Domain compliance PASS. This feature adds a unit-testing suite to a Node CLI; the REST/auth/database/request-validation items on the generic checklist are N/A (no endpoints, DB queries, or auth routes are introduced). Judged against the applicable domain — testing conventions (unit-testing-patterns, gateway-testing) — the suite is exemplary: AAA structure, deterministic, isolated, mock-free with real I/O confined to temp dirs, specific assertions, and no vacuous tests.

checklist.note: No HTTP endpoints added by this feature.
No DB access; CLI persists JSON config to disk only.
All test bodies follow setup → call → assert (e.g. util.test.js readData @file case, config round-trip).

checklist.item: Testing domain — correct mocking boundary (mock I/O, not own logic)
checklist.note: Deliberately mock-free: real temp files (readData, config), real stdout capture (captureOutput), real http server helper. No production logic is stubbed.

checklist.item: Testing domain — test isolation / no shared state
checklist.note: config.test.js uses beforeEach(wipe) + after() cleanup; temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME and HOME restored; node --test runs one process per file so env mutation does not leak.
Exact values via assert.equal/deepEqual and regex on thrown messages; no truthy-only or vacuous assertions.
Names follow [unit] [scenario] [expected], e.g. 'load() returns null when the config file is malformed JSON'.
Port 0 for ephemeral server, env/paths frozen before require, non-TTY assertions gated with node:test skip option.
Only node:test + node:assert/strict and built-in node: modules used.

checklist.item: No real secrets / no writes to real home
checklist.note: All credentials are fake fixtures; XDG_CONFIG_HOME and HOME redirected to temp dirs before src/config require.

warnings: Tests live under top-level test/ rather than co-located *.test.js next to sources. This is an intentional, documented design decision appropriate for node:test and the published files allowlist; recorded as advisory, not a violation.
Coverage threshold (80) is not met by this first pass and is deferred by design (pure/near-pure functions only). Out of scope for domain compliance; will be assessed in Phase 15 (coverage).
